char.name: Leah
char.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Laban
friends.bio: Of Haran. Family is family, and a good deal is a good deal. My sister marries well.

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Reuben
friends.bio: Firstborn of Jacob, eager, but unsteady as water. Found mandrakes in the field for my mother.

friends.name: Simeon
friends.bio: Second son of Leah. Quick to draw the sword when my sister's honor is on the line.

friends.name: Levi
friends.bio: Third son of Leah. Zealous for the family name, sometimes too zealous.

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

friends.name: Dinah
friends.bio: Daughter of Leah and Jacob. I went out to see the women of the land, and everything changed. 💔

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

posts.author: CHAR_172
posts.body: If my husband will not love me, then at least God has seen that I am unloved, and He has opened my womb when my sister's stays shut. I have borne Jacob sons, one after another, and I named each of them with the ache I could never say to his face. Reuben, because I said surely now my husband will love me. Simeon, because the LORD heard that I am hated. Levi, which means attached, because surely NOW, after three sons, he will finally cling to me. And then the fourth son came, and something in me let go of the man at last and reached past him. I named that one Judah, which means praise, and all I said was: this time, I will just praise the LORD. Jacob may never once turn toward me. But God keeps turning toward me. I am learning, slowly, that maybe that was always going to have to be enough.
posts.highlight: the unloved Leah bears Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah; at Judah she turns from craving Jacob to praising God (Gen 29:31-35)
posts.ember: Judah, son of the unloved wife, becomes the royal line to David and to Christ (Gen 49:10; Matt 1); God lifts the overlooked

author_info.name: Leah
author_info.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

posts.author: CHAR_172
posts.body: I am going to say this once, plainly, and then carry it quietly the rest of my days. My father gave me to Jacob in the dark, veiled, on the night that was meant to be my sister's. Seven years the man worked for Rachel, and my father sent ME into that tent instead, and Jacob did not know until the morning light fell on my face. I will go to my grave remembering the way his face changed when he turned and saw it was me, and not her. Something in me died right there on the mat. He was up and out and shouting at my father before the sun was fully up. Why have you deceived me? And my father shrugged and said we do not marry off the younger before the older around here. Finish the bridal week and you can have Rachel too, for another seven years of work. So there it is. I am the wife nobody chose, handed over in the dark to a man who wanted my sister, by a father who used me to cheat him. I know exactly what it is to be a piece in someone else's scheme.
posts.highlight: Laban switches Leah for Rachel on the wedding night; the deceiver Jacob is himself deceived (Gen 29:21-27)
posts.ember: the elder substituted for the younger, exactly the fraud Jacob worked on Isaac; the unloved bride's grief

comments.author: CHAR_175
comments.body: I braided your hair myself that night, my lady. I have never in my life hated a wedding so much. You deserved to be chosen. You still do, and you always will.

author_info.name: Zilpah
author_info.bio: Leah's maidservant, given to Jacob. Adding to the house of Israel.

author_info.name: Leah
author_info.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧
